source_paper_title: Lipschitz Stability of Least-Squares Problems Regularized by Functions with C2-Cone Reducible Conjugates
context:
Section 3, discussion immediately after Theorem 3.8 (around p. 15 in the provided text)

"By fixing the parameter b = \bar b, our condition (45) is also sufficient for the Lipschitz stability of solution mappings S_{\cdot,\bar b,\bar\mu}, S_{\cdot,\bar b,\cdot}, and S_{\bar A,\bar b,\cdot} around the corresponding points due to the implication [(i)\u2192(ii)] in Theorem 3.8. Whether it is necessary is an open question."
title: Necessity of condition (45) for Lipschitz stability when fixing the data vector $b$
latex:
Let \(X,Y\) be Euclidean spaces, \(\bar A\in \mathrm{L}(X,Y)\), \(\bar b\in Y\), and \(\bar\mu>0\). Let \(g:X\to\mathbb{R}\cup\{+\infty\}\) be closed, proper, convex, and assume its Fenchel conjugate \(g^*\) is \(C^2\)-cone reducible at \(\bar z\) defined below.

Consider the regularized least-squares problem
\[
\min_{x\in X}\ \frac{1}{2\bar\mu}\|\bar A x- b\|^2+g(x),
\]
with parameter \(b\in Y\) (and with \(\bar A,\bar\mu\) fixed). Define the solution mapping
\[
S_{\bar A,\bar\mu}(b):=\operatorname*{argmin}_{x\in X}\Big\{\frac{1}{2\bar\mu}\|\bar A x- b\|^2+g(x)\Big\}.
\]
Let \(\bar x\in S_{\bar A,\bar\mu}(\bar b)\) and set
\[
\bar z:=\frac{1}{\bar\mu}\,\bar A^*(\bar b-\bar A\bar x).
\]
Recall that for a closed convex set \(C\subseteq X\), \(\mathrm{par}\,C\) denotes its parallel subspace, and for a convex function \(g^*\) we write \(\partial g^*(\bar z)\) for its subdifferential at \(\bar z\).

Problem: Assume that \(S_{\bar A,\bar\mu}\) is single-valued and locally Lipschitz around \(\bar b\) (i.e., there exist a neighborhood \(U\) of \(\bar b\) and \(L>0\) such that \(S_{\bar A,\bar\mu}(b)\) is a singleton for all \(b\in U\) and \(\|S_{\bar A,\bar\mu}(b_1)-S_{\bar A,\bar\mu}(b_2)\|\le L\|b_1-b_2\|\) for all \(b_1,b_2\in U\)). Must it follow that
\[
\ker \bar A\ \cap\ \mathrm{par}\,\partial g^*(\bar z)=\{0\}? 
\]
Equivalently, is condition \(\ker \bar A\cap \mathrm{par}\,\partial g^*(\bar z)=\{0\}\) necessary (and not only sufficient) for Lipschitz stability of the solution mapping with respect to perturbations in \(b\) alone, when \(\bar A\) and \(\bar\mu\) are fixed and \(g^*\) is \(C^2\)-cone reducible at \(\bar z\)?
location_in_paper: Section 3, discussion immediately after Theorem 3.8 (around p. 15 in the provided text)
open_problem_quote: "By fixing the parameter b = \bar b, our condition (45) is also sufficient for the Lipschitz stability of solution mappings S_{\cdot,\bar b,\bar\mu}, S_{\cdot,\bar b,\cdot}, and S_{\bar A,\bar b,\cdot} around the corresponding points due to the implication [(i)\u2192(ii)] in Theorem 3.8. Whether it is necessary is an open question."
subject_classification: Variational Analysis / Stability (Sensitivity) Analysis in Convex Optimization
